reasoning_model_1: Model 1 perfectly follows the user's command to speak in a 'whisper volume' (耳语的音量). From 00:00 to 00:09, the audio is completely unvoiced, characterized by the breathy, aspirate quality of a true whisper without vocal cord vibration. The content matches the provided script exactly: '这份文件非常机密...'. The pacing and intonation are highly natural, effectively conveying the secretive atmosphere required by the text. The paralinguistic feature is vivid and accurate.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails to execute the core instruction of 'whisper volume'. The audio generated (00:00-00:08) uses normal phonation (voiced speech with vocal cord vibration) rather than a whisper. While the tone is somewhat playful or secretive, it is acoustically a normal speaking voice, not a whisper. Furthermore, the model hallucinates and modifies the text significantly: it adds '嘘' (Shh) at the start, changes '不要' to '别', and adds '懂吗？' (Understand?) at the end. These are major content errors. The failure to whisper and the script deviations result in a very poor evaluation.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It accurately generates the requested 'whisper' paralinguistic feature, producing unvoiced, breathy speech that perfectly fits the 'secretive' context of the script. It also adheres strictly to the text. Model 2 fails on both counts: it generates normal voiced speech instead of a whisper, and it significantly alters the script by adding and changing words. Model 1 demonstrates superior control over paralinguistic features and content fidelity.
